vitals.date_specific: 2026 Oaxaca leg: cancelled (listed 19 April 2026, marked "Fecha anulada")
vitals.start_format: Mass start, central Oaxaca de Juárez
vitals.entry_fee: Historical (2024 registration page, unconfirmed as run): MXN 1,000 early / 1,300 advance / 1,500 regular / 2,500 at expo

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 70. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 66. WAVE 6 (2026-07-24): climbing corrected 4->2 after research sourced the organizer's own 2022 Strava route at 703m total climbing (rubric band 2, 'Gentle: 500-1,500m') — the prior climbing=4 implied 2,500-4,000m, which is unsupported by any source found. Score recalculated: 66 -> 63 (provisional). Note: the previously-filed 109.4km distance figure (superseded by the sourced 103.45km value above) also has no traceable source. altitude=4 left unchanged pending a peak-elevation source (Oaxaca valley floor sits ~1,550m already; no specific route-peak figure was found this wave — flagged in catalog_flags for a human to verify against a GPX if one surfaces).

course_description.character: In the confirmed 2022 and 2023 editions, the Oaxaca leg started in central Oaxaca de Juárez, following Avenida Independencia/Boulevard Eduardo Vasconcelos onto Federal Highway 190 before splitting into 70 km and 100-110 km options — distances that shifted between those two editions rather than holding to a fixed course. In 2022, the short route turned back near Yaguar Xoo/Tlacolula and the long route continued through Tlacolula and Santiago Matatlán toward Nueve Puntas, returning on Highway 190 to a finish at the Eduardo Vasconcelos baseball stadium; the 2023 edition added a documented detour through Mitla. The organizer's own 2022 Strava route for the long course measures 103.45 km with 703 m of climbing — the most reliably sourced distance/elevation pair for this event.
course_description.signature_challenge: Nueve Puntas — the long course's turnaround point and the site of the timed competitive segment in the 2022 edition.
suffering_zones: Federal Highway 190 — the main paved out-and-back artery, exposed to Oaxacan valley heat and highway traffic
Nueve Puntas — the timed competitive segment and turnaround for the long course
The Mitla detour (2023 edition only) — added distance and a pass through the archaeological-site town, illustrating how much the route varied year to year

logistics.lodging_strategy: Hotels and guesthouses abundant in Oaxaca de Juárez; book early for event weekend.

history.origin_story: The Oaxaca leg debuted in February 2022, billed at the time as the tenth anniversary of the national Raúl Alcalá Challenge series (founded 2012) and Oaxaca's first time hosting a stop. The series honors retired Mexican professional Raúl Alcalá Gallegos, a two-time Tour de France stage winner (1989 and 1990), and rotates its multi-city calendar (Oaxaca, CDMX, Morelos, León, Monterrey in 2022; Oaxaca, Morelos, Monterrey as a package by 2024; San Luis Potosí, Morelos, Nuevo León in 2025) — Oaxaca has since dropped off the rotation.

biased_opinion.verdict: 2026 Oaxaca Date Cancelled — Parent Series Continued Elsewhere in 2025
biased_opinion.summary: The only Oaxaca-specific 2026 listing found for the Raúl Alcalá Challenge is marked 'Fecha anulada' (date cancelled) on a third-party cycling calendar; no organizer notice was found either way. This is not evidence the whole series has folded: the parent series continued elsewhere in 2025 — San Luis Potosí held an April 2025 edition drawing 2,000+ riders — and remains listed in Sport City's 2026 program, subject to a confirmed date. Oaxaca itself, however, has no confirmed or completed 2026 edition. The last confirmed completed Oaxaca edition was 2023, drawing roughly 500 riders across 70/110 km courses.
strengths: Named for Raúl Alcalá, who won Tour de France stages in 1989 and 1990 and remains the only Mexican rider with two Tour stage wins
Grew from 300 riders at its 2022 debut to roughly 500 in 2023 — modest but real momentum across its two confirmed editions
State-government-backed (Instituto del Deporte del Estado de Oaxaca) with both road and MTB bikes welcomed, lowering the entry bar
2024 fee schedule (MXN 1,000-2,500 depending on tier) is the last available pricing reference, if the leg returns
weaknesses: No confirmed or completed 2026 Oaxaca edition — the only specific 2026 listing found is marked cancelled on a third-party calendar
Course distances were not stable across its two confirmed editions (70/100 km in 2022, 70/110 km in 2023)
A 300-500 rider field across its confirmed editions — a small, regional-scale event
The public record is incomplete: a 2024 registration page exists, but no post-event report or results for a 2024 edition were verified
biased_opinion.bottom_line: Not currently bookable in Oaxaca for 2026. If you want to ride in Alcalá's honor, check which city currently holds a confirmed date in the parent series rather than planning a trip to Oaxaca around this name.

final_verdict.one_liner: A tribute ride to a genuine two-time Tour de France stage winner — but no confirmed 2026 edition in Oaxaca.
final_verdict.should_you_race: Not in Oaxaca for 2026 — no confirmed or completed edition was found there. If you want to ride in Alcalá's honor, check which city currently holds a confirmed date in the parent series.
final_verdict.alternatives: The wider Raúl Alcalá Challenge series (run under Emoción Deportiva/Sport City), which held 2025 editions in San Luis Potosí, Morelos, and Nuevo León rather than Oaxaca — verify current-year city assignments before assuming Oaxaca is on the calendar.

youtube_data.search_query: Raul Alcala Challenge Oaxaca gran fondo cycling Oaxaca de Juárez

videos.title: Vuelve a vibrar el ciclismo en Oaxaca con el Raúl Alcalá Challenge
videos.description:
OAXACA,  Oax. 21 de febrero de 2022.- Con más de 300 pedalistas, en seis diferentes categorías de ambas ramas, se realizó la carrera Raúl Alcalá Challenge, con salida en la Alameda de León y llegada en el estadio Eduardo Vasconcelos.
El Raúl Alcalá Challenge es un evento deportivo para entusiastas del ciclismo, desde principiantes hasta semi-profesionales y ofrece una experiencia única para que los fanáticos y amigos se unan al doble ganador de etapas en el Tour de Francia Raúl “El Duende” Alcalá.
Ciclistas oaxaqueños participantes comentaron que es muy importante hacer este tipo de eventos pues atrae al turismo nacional.
Oaxaca fue la primera fecha de este año 2022, misma que ganó Fernando Arroyo Rosario, hijo de El Halcón de Huamantla, Miguel Arroyo, en representación de Tlaxcala, dejando en segundo lugar a Armando García y en tercero a Manuel López Robles; ambos de Oaxaca, en los 100 kilómetros.
En femenil ganó Valeria Sáenz Miranda que al igual que los varones, compitió en la Libre para 24-39 años que es la más competitiva.
El reto que seguirá  es la Ciudad de México, Morelos, León y Monterrey.

videos.title: Fiesta del ciclismo en Oaxaca con Raúl Alcalá
videos.description:
Buscando vivir el ciclismo desde su esencia, disfrutando rodar sin la presión de ganar pero sí con el impulso de superarse a si mismo, así fue que 300 ciclistas participaron en la primera edición del “Raúl Alcalá Challenge 2022” en Oaxaca, el cual realizó este domingo en las distancias de 70 y 100 kilómetros. 
Después de una pausa prolongada por la pandemia, ciclistas de varias partes del país participaron en la primera edición de la carrera ciclista “Raúl Alcalá Challenge 2022”, actividad que significó la reactivación del ciclismo después de la pausa impuesta por la pandemia por Covid-19.

videos.title: Raúl Alcalá Challenge
videos.description: El día de ayer se llevó a cabo la Segunda Edición del "Raúl Alcalá Challenge" a cargo del Instituto del Deporte del Estado de Oaxaca, con la finalidad de impulsar el deporte en el estado y poder reconocer al ciclista Raúl Alcalá.

videos.title: Alcalá Challenge en Oaxaca 70 y 100 kms.
videos.description: Gran rodada de convivencia con el ciclista Raúl Alcalá en Oaxaca. #oaxaca #ciclismo #ciclismoderuta

eligibility.notes: The only Oaxaca-specific 2026 listing found (19 April 2026, on a third-party cycling calendar, not an organizer notice) explicitly reads 'Fecha anulada' (date cancelled) — no confirmed or completed 2026 Oaxaca edition found. This does not indicate a series shutdown: the parent Raúl Alcalá Challenge continued elsewhere in 2025 (San Luis Potosí held an April 2025 edition drawing 2,000+ riders) and remains listed in Sport City's 2026 program, subject to a confirmed date. Last confirmed completed Oaxaca edition: 2023.

catalog_flags.status_note: Oaxaca leg cancelled for 2026 per official calendar listing; parent series (Raúl Alcalá Challenge, founded 2012) remains active in other Mexican cities (San Luis Potosí, Morelos, Nuevo León as of the 2025 rotation). Recommend a human decide whether this profile stays in the active catalog as a cancelled/dormant leg or should be pruned, consistent with how other cancelled-status profiles in this database are handled. Also flagging fondo_rating.altitude=4 as unverified this wave: Oaxaca de Juárez's valley floor already sits at roughly 1,550m, and no specific route peak-elevation figure was found (only total climbing, 703m, was sourced) — a human with access to a GPX trace should confirm whether altitude=4 ('high passes 2,000-2,500m') is accurate or should be revised alongside the climbing correction already made this wave.
